title: Binary Search Trees
description: Searching, deleting from, and analysing the performance of binary search trees, building on the BST insertion and traversal covered in Data Structures.
objectives: Trace the BST search algorithm and explain why it discards half the remaining tree at each step
Delete a node from a BST in each of the three cases: leaf, one child, and two children (using the in-order successor)
Explain why BST search, insertion and deletion are O(log n) in the best case but O(n) in the worst case
Explain how insertion order determines a BST's shape, and why a degenerate (skewed) tree loses the log n advantage
Describe, at a conceptual level, why self-balancing trees exist and what problem they solve

lessons.title: Deleting from a binary search tree
